title: Academic Degree Requirements Core
content:
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science - Degree Requirements
Morgan State University

---

DEGREE OVERVIEW
A minimum of 120 credit hours are required to graduate with a B.S. in Computer Science.

Credit Distribution:
- General Education and University Requirements: 44 credits
- Supporting Courses: 11 credits
- Required Courses for the Computer Science Major: 65 credits
- Total Credits: 120

---

SUPPORTING COURSES (11 credits)

MATH 241 - Calculus I
Credits: 4

MATH 242 - Calculus II
Credits: 4

MATH 312 - Linear Algebra I
Credits: 3

MATH 331 - Applied Probability and Statistics
Credits: 3

---

REQUIRED CORE COURSES

COSC 111 - Introduction to Computer Science I
Credits: 4
Prerequisite: None
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Introduction to programming concepts and problem-solving using a high-level programming language.

COSC 112 - Introduction to Computer Science II
Credits: 4
Prerequisite: COSC 111 with a grade of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Continuation of COSC 111, covering advanced programming concepts and data structures.

COSC 201 - Computer Ethics
Credits: 1
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Ethical issues in computing and professional responsibility.

COSC 220 - Data Structures and Algorithms
Credits: 4
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Study of fundamental data structures and algorithms for efficient problem-solving.

COSC 241 - Computer Systems and Digital Logic
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 112 and MATH 241 with grades of "C" or higher, or departmental permission
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Introduction to computer organization and digital logic design.

COSC 281 - Discrete Structures
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Mathematical foundations for computer science including logic, sets, and combinatorics.

COSC 349 - Computer Networks
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Fundamentals of computer networking, protocols, and network architecture.

COSC 351 - Cybersecurity
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Introduction to cybersecurity principles and practices.

COSC 352 - Organization of Programming Languages
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Study of programming language concepts, paradigms, and design principles.

COSC 354 - Operating Systems
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 and COSC 241 with grades of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Principles of operating system design and implementation.

COSC 458 - Software Engineering
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Software development methodologies and project management.

COSC 459 - Database Design
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher
Offered: Fall/Spring
Description: Database design, SQL, relational models, and database management systems.

COSC 490 - Senior Project
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: Department chair permission and senior standing
Offered: As needed
Description: Capstone project demonstrating mastery of computer science concepts.

---

ELECTIVE COURSES

Students must choose electives from four groups to complete degree requirements.

GROUP A ELECTIVES (Choose 3 courses)

COSC 238 - Object-Oriented Programming
Credits: 4
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 239 - Java Programming
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 243 - Computer Architecture
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 241 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 251 - Introduction to Data Science
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher

CLCO 261 - Introduction to Cloud Computing
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher

GROUP B ELECTIVES (Choose 2 courses)

COSC 320 - Algorithm Design and Analysis
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 323 - Introduction to Cryptography
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 332 - Introduction to Game Design and Development
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 338 - Mobile App Design and Development
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 383 - Numerical Methods and Programming
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 385 - Theory of Languages and Automata
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 281 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 386 - Introduction to Quantum Computing
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 281 with a grade of "C" or higher

EEGR 317 - Electronic Circuits
Credits: 4
Prerequisite: See EEGR department

GROUP C ELECTIVES (Choose 4 courses)

COSC 460 - Computer Graphics
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 470 - Artificial Intelligence
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 472 - Introduction to Machine Learning
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 112 with a grade of "C" or higher, MATH 312 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 480 - Introduction to Image Processing and Analysis
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 220 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 486 - Applied Quantum Computing
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: COSC 386 with a grade of "C" or higher

COSC 491 - Conference Course
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: Department permission

COSC 498 - Senior Internship
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: Senior standing and department permission

COSC 499 - Senior Research or Teaching/Tutorial Assistantship
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: Senior standing and department permission

CLCO 471 - Data Analytics in Cloud
Credits: 3
Prerequisite: CLCO 261 with a grade of "C" or higher

GROUP D ELECTIVES (Choose 1 course)

INSS 391 - IT Infrastructure and Security
Credits: 3

INSS 494 - Information Security and Risk Management
Credits: 3

EEGR 481 - Introduction to Network Security
Credits: 3

EEGR 483 - Introduction to Security Management
Credits: 3
